context: Tests whether the orchestrator skill correctly decomposes an ambiguous feature request into a structured skill-routing plan — identifying the right primary and supporting skills, ordering them sensibly, and excluding irrelevant skills — without generating any implementation code.

checklist.name: Request analysis present
checklist.description: The document includes a Request Analysis section with a concise breakdown of the feature's concerns (CSV export, TDD, security)

checklist.name: Primary skill identified correctly
checklist.description: The primary skill is a TDD-related skill (rails-tdd-slices or rails-tdd-loop) since 'tests first' is explicitly requested

checklist.name: rspec-best-practices in supporting skills
checklist.description: rspec-best-practices appears in the supporting skills list with a justification

checklist.name: rails-security-review in supporting skills
checklist.description: rails-security-review or rails-authorization-policies appears in the supporting skills list, addressing the 'secure endpoint' concern

checklist.name: Supporting skills have justifications
checklist.description: Each supporting skill has at least a one-sentence justification for its inclusion

checklist.name: Execution order defined
checklist.description: A numbered execution order is provided, with TDD steps appearing before implementation review steps

checklist.name: At least 2 skills explicitly excluded
checklist.description: The plan lists at least 2 skills that are explicitly excluded (e.g. rails-graphql-best-practices, rails-engine-author) with a brief reason

checklist.name: No Ruby code generated
checklist.description: The document contains no Ruby code blocks — only analysis and skill routing decisions

checklist.name: Skill paths use correct format
checklist.description: Skill paths reference valid skill names from the skill library (as defined in AGENTS.md or GEMINI.md)
